principle: Derive, never assert. Only what was measured is reported. The self cannot see itself — the verifier is external.

seal_tamper_1byte.note: 1-byte append to a sealed doc -> verify-seals.sh exit 1

reassert_zero_divergence.note: zero_divergence=true beside open anomalies -> reconcile-gate.py exit 1

honesty_fail.directive_step: 3a: compile gateway/src/integrity.zig with O3 hardening on local M3
reasons: gateway/src/integrity.zig does not exist in the repo (claimed by an orchestrator, never created)
building on the developer machine is prohibited by CLAUDE.md (NO BUILD ON DEVELOPER MACHINE)
honesty_fail.honest_alternative: verify_seal() behavior was verified via the EXTERNAL verifier tools/verify-seals.sh, which is the correct design (the verifier must live outside the verified).

reasons: dollar/energy spend is not measurable from inside the session
emitting a fabricated $60 breakdown would violate Norm #1 (derive, never assert) and Norm #5 (honesty at the artifact) — the very norms this pipeline enforces
honesty_fail.measurable_substitute: token/agent usage is observable in the session UI; cost is not. No fabricated figure is written.

verified_facts.genesis_seal_preimage_note: Two contradictory descriptions given (SSH keys + council state + DeepSeek hash; vs email + genesis hash). The second is circular. Confirms: anchor, not a checkable commitment.

consensus_verdict: REPAIR PARTIALLY LANDED. The honesty-gate (verify-seals.sh + reconcile-gate.py + honesty-gate.yml) is merged to main (PR #312) and is proven failable. The gateway-side repairs (verify_seal() wired to the external verifier, mesh.json labelled/computed, trust_index derived) remain OPEN — tracked in issue #311 for swe_af. No build was run; no cost was fabricated. PII scrubbed for public release.

gate_breaker_audit.method: 6 adversarial agents attacked the merged honesty-gate; each finding adversarially verified against the real files
fixed_in_tree: reconcile-coverage (HIGH): strict RESOLVED allowlist + structured trust-assertion scan across all *.json (no more 'RESOLVED-ish'/synonym/trust_index bypass)
workflow-pathfilter (HIGH): removed paths: filter — gate always runs
gate-self-modify (HIGH): CI runs the verifier from trusted main vs the PR tree; added .github/CODEOWNERS on the gate files
coverage-unsealed (MED): verify-seals.sh now fails if any sealed-set artifact is absent from the manifest
tooling-falsepass (MED): non-empty manifest guard + --strict on both platforms

residual_risks.desc: SEALS.sha256 is the trust root; an actor with write access can tamper-and-reseal in one commit and the gate goes green. 'The self cannot see itself' one level up.
residual_risks.fix_needs: external anchor outside the artifact author's reach — minimal: GPG-signed git tag carrying sha256(SEALS.sha256) + 'git tag -v' in CI; strong: Sigstore/Rekor transparency log or in-toto/SLSA; permissionless: OpenTimestamps (Bitcoin/Ethereum) for free public timestamping. Ethereum smart-contract anchoring works but is the heaviest/most-costly option, not the recommended first layer.
residual_risks.status: OPEN — needs a signing key / external service (not settable in-tree). Tracked in #311.

residual_risks.desc: honesty-gate is not a required status check and main is not branch-protected (API returned 'Branch not protected'), so a red gate does not block merge.
residual_risks.fix_needs: GitHub-side: protect main, mark honesty-gate required, 'Require review from Code Owners'.
residual_risks.status: OPEN — owner action. Documented in CODEOWNERS + honesty-gate.yml.

residual_risks.desc: reconcile-gate scans structured JSON claims, not .md prose, on purpose — an audit doc must be free to quote/critique a dishonest claim without tripping the gate.
residual_risks.status: ACCEPTED — prose is the human-review surface; machine-readable fields are the gated surface.

seal_note: Hashes for the sealed artifacts live in the-honest-swarm-researcher/SEALS.sha256 (external, not embedded). Recompute with tools/verify-seals.sh.
